tables: message = ''This is information about Potions in Slay the Spire. A full searchable list can be found at [[StS2:Potions List
wikitext:
{{Sequel Disambiguation}}
{{Ambox
| color = var(--highlight-color-shop)
| icon = StS2_ClarityExtract.png
| message = ''This is information about '''Potions''' in Slay the Spire. A full searchable list can be found at [[StS2:Potions List|Potions List (Slay the Spire 2)]].''
}} {{#setmainimage:StS2_ClarityExtract.png|quiet}}

Potions are single-use consumables that persist across floors.

Most potions provide a temporary boost that can only be used in a single encounter, and thus can only be used in combat. The only potions that can be used outside of combat are: {{P|Entropic Brew||2}}, {{P|Foul Potion||2}} (only when used against the {{2|Merchant}}), {{P|Fruit Juice||2}}, and {{P|Blood Potion||2}} (Ironclad only).

Potions are stored in Potion Slots, of which you start with 3 ({{Asc|4|2|2}}). The only ways to increase the amount of slots you have is to obtain {{R|Potion Belt||2}}, {{R|Alchemical Coffer||2}}, or {{R|Phial Holster||2}}.

Using a potion does not cost {{KW|Energy||2}} and does not count as playing a card.

Some potions are exclusive to a single character. Each character has 3 unique potions, with one for each rarity.

== Potion Rarity ==
In most cases, including playing {{C|Alchemize||2}}, the rarity chances for obtaining potions are:
{| class="wikitable"
|Common Potion
|65%
|-
|Uncommon Potion
|25%
|-
|Rare Potion
|10%
|}

== Sources ==
Potions may be dropped as a reward after combat. The '''default chance''' of a potion dropping is '''40%''' at the start of a run. <!-- !!Commented out until it can be confirmed!! and resets at the start of each [[StS2:Acts|Act]]. -->

If a potion drops, the chance '''decreases''' by '''10%'''. If a potion doesn't drop, the chance '''increases''' by '''10%.''' 
* With {{R|White Beast Statue||2}}, a potion will always drop after combat. This decreases the drop chances as well.
* {{2|Elites}} have an '''increased''' chance of obtaining a potion by '''12.5%.''' 
** There is currently a bug where if you would normally not have gotten a potion reward, but did get one with the increased Elite potion chances, the game still increases the odds for the next potion.

=== Cards ===
* Using the card {{C|Alchemize||2}} creates 1 random potion (cannot be {{P|Fruit Juice||2}}).

=== Relics ===
* Obtaining the {{R|Alchemical Coffer||2}} from {{KW|Orobas||2}} gives you 4 extra potion slots, each filled with a random potion.
* Obtaining the {{R|Lost Coffer||2}} from {{KW|Neow||2}} gives you a potion as well as a card reward.
* Obtaining the {{R|Phial Holster||2}}  from {{KW|Neow||2}} gives you an extra potion slot as well as 2 potions.
* Obtaining the {{R|Delicate Frond||2}} from {{KW|Nonupeipe||2}} fills each empty potion slot with a random potion at the beginning of combat.
* {{R|Cauldron}} gives you 5 random potions.
* {{R|Tiny Mailbox||2}} gives a random potion every time you Rest at a {{2|Rest Site}}.
* {{R|Petrified Toad}} gives you one {{P|Potion-Shaped Rock||2}} at the beginning of combat.

=== Other ===
* {{P|Entropic Brew||2}} fills all your empty potion slots, including its slot, with random potions (cannot generate {{P|Fruit Juice||2}} while in combat).
* Potions can also be bought at the {{2|Merchant}}.

== Interactions ==
=== Synergies ===
* {{R|Reptile Trinket||2}} gives 3 temporary {{BD|Strength||2}} when using a potion.
* {{R|The Courier||2}} makes it so the {{2|Merchant}} will never run out of potions.

=== Neutral ===
* {{R|Belt Buckle||2}} gives 2 {{BD|Dexterity||2}} while you have no potions.

=== Anti-Synergies ===
* Obtaining {{R|Sozu||2}} from {{KW|Darv||2}} prevents you from obtaining any potions. Any potions you had before picking this up will still be kept.
